picks.reason: Gasser is [sm] flagged (24 IP) and Elder's strong ERA masks an ugly 4.04 xFIP/4.23 SIERA with Stf+ 85 — both arms below true-talent quality, so my numbers don't clear the side gate either way. My xR sits near 8.8 vs posted 8.5; Over price is stale-flagged anyway. No clean edge.

Burns (3.29 SIERA, 21.6 K-BB%, elite arm) is the better starter, but Rodríguez is a disaster — 6.44 SIERA, -4.7% K-BB%, [sm] — facing a strong NYY offense in a HR park. The market has this near pick'em on the moneyline because Burns' name and the Reds' "form" distort it; I make NYY ~57% true vs ~52% implied. Total near my number, no totals edge. Capped at 1u because Rodríguez's awful line rests on a thin 13 IP sample.
picks.cc_cited_fact: NYY lineup confirmed with Rice (178 vs RHP) leading off and Bellinger (138) batting third; Reds order unchanged. Line moved -112→-110, a slight improvement on my number. The edge (Rodríguez 6.44 SIERA on thin sample vs Burns) is intact and still rests on the same [sm] sample, keeping it at 1u.

picks.reason: The 201-pt ML move flags potential roster/lineup staleness — data-integrity caution applies, I won't anchor a play on possibly-stale pitcher data. Martin is the better arm but the line already moved toward CHW. xR near 8.0 vs posted 8.0, no totals gap.

Capped at 1u — Gusto's line is small-sample though the talent gap is real.
picks.cc_cited_fact: SF confirmed lineup matches Run-1 read exactly — vs_RHP 118 wRC+ intact (Eldridge 156, Lee 143, Devers 110); MIA still leads off Marsee (83 wRC+) with Conine excluded on low PA. ML unchanged at -144.

picks.reason: Martinez's 2.60 ERA badly overstates his 4.66 SIERA, and Alvarez is [sm] with command volatility (6.8 BB/9 L14). Both arms are below their surface ERAs, but that roughly cancels at the line; TB ~57% implied feels about right. Dome neutralizes weather; xR ~8.3 vs 8.0, no totals edge.

Cecconi and Teng are roughly matched mid-tier arms (4.2-4.4 SIERA), HOU's bullpen is far fresher (T1/T2 fully available vs CLE 0/0), which nudges toward HOU but not enough to clear 4 pts. xR near 8.5 with thunderstorm/gust variance; possible rain delay risk. No edge.

May is in a clear true-talent groove (3.76 SIERA, 26 K-BB% over last 15 IP, zero-walk profile) and outclasses Kolek (4.15 SIERA, 12.1 K-BB%); STL's bats vs RHP (123 wRC+) are the better unit. I make STL ~60% vs ~56% implied. The 16+ mph wind with 26 mph gusts is a totals variance flag and the run gap is thin anyway, so no total. 1u — sound but moderate edge.

picks.reason: SD starter is TBD — mandatory data-integrity pass.

Coors Field — my method auto-passes Coors totals (Runs factor 130, extreme variance) and the side rests on two volatile arms (Jones [sm], Lorenzen 4.45 SIERA) in a chaos environment. No play either market.

Detmers (3.35 SIERA, 21.4 K-BB%, 14 IP of zero-walk dominance) is a clearly superior arm to Perkins (4.06 SIERA, [sm], 5.0 BB/9 L14), and ATH's bullpen is gutted (T1 0/3, T3 1/5) in a hitter park where games stay close. I make LAA ~53% vs ~48% implied — getting plus money on the better team. Lean Over (Sutter Health 121 Runs, xR ~9.9 vs 9.5) but below the 0.8 gate. 1u dog with real edge.
picks.cc_cited_fact: Confirmed lineups match Run-1 reads exactly — ATH vs_LHP 129 CFM (Heim 181, Kurtz 134), LAA vs_RHP 110 CFM unchanged; ML stayed +108 with no line move. Detmers' 3.35 SIERA / 21.4 K-BB% edge over Perkins (4.06 SIERA [sm]) and the gutted ATH pen (0/3, 1/5) all hold.

picks.reason: LAD heavily favored at -215, but Young's surface ERA (3.18) hides an ugly 4.87 SIERA and 2.0% K-BB% L14, while Sheehan (3.43 SIERA) is the better arm and LAD has the deeper offense and pen — the price already reflects all of it. No edge at -215, and the -1.5 RL at -102 doesn't give me enough either. xR near my number.

Tolle (3.71 SIERA, Stf+ 115) and Gilbert (3.45 SIERA, 20.8 K-BB%, elite) are both quality arms; SEA's slight edge at home (~58% implied) is fair, and BOS bullpen is depleted (T1 0/1). Both starters anchor a low total — my xR ~6.7 vs posted 6.5, no totals gap. Pitcher-friendly park reinforces the low number but no actionable edge.

Wheeler (3.38 SIERA, ace-level, dominant L14) is rightly a heavy favorite over Peterson (4.37 SIERA, [sm] AGG, 5.91 ERA), and PHI at -196 already prices the gap fully. The -1.5 RL at +110 is interesting but Peterson's GB tendency and NYM's competent pen keep blowout odds modest — not enough edge. xR near posted 8.0.
